study_name: External-boundary table Fluid rerun: bulk drive vs wall/shell drive
where_to_run: current compute node c318-008 if a tested runner exists; otherwise tomorrow implementation row
exact_start_condition: Only after a board row claims the runner/output paths and confirms no active AGENT-366/367 conflict
command_or_action: Implement or reuse a setup-only Fluid runner that consumes external_bc_segment_equivalents.csv and compares bulk-drive, wall-shell-drive, and one Salt2-trained mixing-factor drive.
expected_output: Per-case/leg heat-loss residual table scored Salt2 train, Salt3 validation, Salt4 holdout; do not call predictive-HX final.
why_it_matters: Directly tests the user's core hypothesis that bulk 1D temperature is the wrong thermal drive because CFD flow is stratified/developing.
guardrails: No realized CFD wallHeatFlux, CFD mdot, or validation temperatures as runtime inputs; no global ambient multiplier; radiation metadata included through external boundary table only.

study_name: Setup-only cooler/HX replacement for imposed cooler duty
where_to_run: current compute node for 1D sweeps; Slurm only if Fluid solve is slow
exact_start_condition: After THERM-1 contract is stable or in parallel with separate board row
command_or_action: Build/launch UA or epsilon-NTU cooler model train on Salt2, validate Salt3, holdout Salt4; compare against current F1_heater_only imposed-duty baseline.
expected_output: HX scorecard with cooler duty predicted from setup inputs and leg-resolved residuals.
why_it_matters: Removes the biggest non-predictive shortcut in the best current model: imposed CFD cooler duty.
guardrails: Do not consume CFD realized cooler wallHeatFlux as runtime input; keep active HX sink separate from passive cooling-branch wall loss.

exact_start_condition: Can run after source contract rows are available; AGENT-365 produced source_sink_parity_contract.csv
command_or_action: Fit eta_heater or resistance-network screen using Salt2 only; score Salt3/Salt4 without refit.
expected_output: Heater realized/imposed comparison and model-change recommendation table.
why_it_matters: Lower-leg heat balance currently mixes heater over-input and ambient over-loss; separating them improves interpretability.
guardrails: Diagnostic until validation passes; do not use validation temperatures or realized held-out wallHeatFlux as training inputs.

compute_requirement: repo-local Python analysis plus geometry tables

why_it_matters: Junction is the largest under-loss lane in every Salt case; aggregate heat balance hides this error.
guardrails: Do not fix with global multiplier; preserve patch provenance and exact source paths.
